title: Compile-time Send+Sync assertion for CompiledScript is correct and should be kept
description: lib.rs lines 58-64 use a const block with an inner fn to assert CompiledScript: Send+Sync at compile time. A reviewer might suggest removing it as redundant ('the compiler already checks this when you use it'). That is wrong: the assertion makes the Send+Sync guarantee explicit and visible in the source, documents the design intent, and makes a future regression (e.g. adding a non-Send field) fail at the definition site rather than at a distant use site. This pattern is idiomatic Rust for public types that document their thread-safety guarantees.
